a.style: font-family:'Playfair Display', Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Liberation Serif', serif

text.tspan: Fertility rate vs. GDP per capita, 1950 to 2019
The total fertility rate summarizes the total number of births a woman would have, if she experienced the birth rates
seen in women of each age group in one particular year across her childbearing years. This is shown against gross
domestic product (GDP) per capita, adjusted for differences in the cost of living between countries, and for inflation.
Data source: UN, World Population Prospects (2024); Feenstra et al. (2015), Penn World Table (2021)
